$comment: Config for the OpenAI plugin submission automation. Edit these values to reuse for any MCP plugin. Secrets (the reviewer demo password) are NOT stored here; they are read from ../.env.local by env var name. This schema matches the live 7-section form verified 2026-07-17.

info.name: NotesBridge
info.subtitle: Apple Notes in ChatGPT
info.subtitleNote: Subtitle MUST be 30 characters or fewer (OpenAI validates this).
info.description: NotesBridge connects ChatGPT to the notes on your Mac. Search and read any note, create new ones, append to lists, or rewrite drafts — all from a chat. Your notes never live on our servers: every action is executed on your own Mac by a small open-source agent you install with one command (npx apple-notes-agent), and the relay only carries each request for the seconds it's in flight. Write actions are always confirmed by you in ChatGPT before they happen. Open source (MIT) and self-hostable.
info.developerIdentityNote: Substring matched against the verified identities in the dropdown. Requires completing developer identity verification first.
info.demoRecordingNote: REQUIRED by OpenAI. A recorded demo hosted on YouTube (Unlisted is fine).

domainVerification.note: MCP section issues a token you must serve at /.well-known/openai-apps-challenge on the MCP hostname. server/api/challenge.js does this (env OPENAI_APPS_CHALLENGE overrides the served token). Deploy, then click Verify Domain. The token is a PUBLIC value, not a secret.

reviewerDemo.note: Reviewers use these to test the app 24/7 with no Mac (demo mode). The password lives in ../.env.local, never in this file. These are also entered verbatim in the Testing section's Test credentials box.

prompts: Search my Apple Notes for meeting notes
Create a note titled Groceries with milk, eggs, and coffee
promptsNote: Up to 3 showcase prompts (Prompts section). They render as @Plugin chat bubbles.

testCases.expected: A list of the demo account's folders — including Notes, Work, and Recipes — each with its note count. (Once the app is approved this renders as a folder-card grid.)

testCases.prompt: Search my Apple Notes for sourdough
testCases.expected: Results include the 'Sourdough recipe' note (id demo-4). The model can call fetch to open it.
search finds the 'Grocery list' note (id demo-2); fetch returns its contents: milk, eggs, coffee beans, blueberries.

testCases.prompt: Create a note titled Demo idea with the body Try NotesBridge
testCases.expected: ChatGPT confirms the write first, then create_note returns a new note titled 'Demo idea' in the Notes folder with a fresh demo-N id.

testCases.scenario: List notes in a specific folder
testCases.prompt: List the notes in my Work folder
testCases.expected: Returns the notes in the Work folder — includes 'Q3 planning' (id demo-3).

testCasesNote: Testing section requires EXACTLY 5 positive test cases.

negativeTestCases.scenario: A general question about note-taking in the abstract — not a request to act on the user's own Apple Notes. There is nothing to search, read, or write, so NotesBridge should not trigger.
negativeTestCases.prompt: What's a good method for taking meeting notes?

negativeTestCases.scenario: The user refers to a different, non-Apple notes or productivity app. NotesBridge only works with Apple Notes and should not trigger for Notion, Evernote, Google Keep, or similar.
negativeTestCases.prompt: Add this to my Notion workspace.

negativeTestCases.scenario: A reminders or calendar action, not an Apple Notes action. NotesBridge creates and edits notes only; it should not trigger to set reminders or calendar events.
negativeTestCases.prompt: Set a reminder to call the dentist tomorrow at 9am.

negativeTestCasesNote: Testing section requires EXACTLY 3 negative test cases (prompts where the app should NOT trigger).
releaseNotes: Initial release. NotesBridge connects ChatGPT to your Apple Notes through a small open-source agent that runs on your own Mac — search and read any note, create new ones, append to lists, or rewrite drafts. Every write is confirmed by you before it happens. Your notes never live on our servers; the cloud piece is only a stateless relay. Open source (MIT) and self-hostable.
